steps.title: Put terms on one side
steps.content:
Move the right side to the left side.
$$
2x^2-2=2x+3
$$
$$
2x^2-2x-5=0
$$

steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content:
Use the quadratic formula with $a=2$, $b=-2$, and $c=-5$.
$$
x=\frac{2\pm\sqrt{(-2)^2-4(2)(-5)}}{2(2)}
$$
$$
x=\frac{2\pm\sqrt{44}}{4}=\frac{1\pm\sqrt{11}}{2}
$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graphing $y=2x^2-2$ and $y=2x+3$ gives the same intersection $x$-values. The listed solution with the plus sign matches the choices. The answer is D.
